issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason:
Adversarial re-verify (S14) — IMPORTANT correction. I measured the REAL bottleneck on prod and it changes the fix:
- A single /ai/suggest-meal call is ~14s. But 3 fired CONCURRENTLY each balloon to ~40-53s — the LLM endpoint throttles concurrent requests per account ~3-4x.
- So my earlier 'concurrent warm' change (bd674198) was based on a wrong assumption (that parallel warming finishes in ~one LLM round). It does NOT — it makes every slot contend and delays the whole deck to ~50s+ with nothing ready early. I REVERTED it (25c5bd2c) back to the original SEQUENTIAL warm, which keeps each call fast (~14s) and fills the deck incrementally (breakfast ~14s / lunch ~28s / dinner ~42s) — a better cache-hit rate for the build after the user's dwell on the workout builder.
- Net: the genuine remaining wait is the BACKEND /ai/suggest-meal latency itself (~14s/call, worse under any concurrency), NOT client-side warm ordering. That's an owner/infra call — options: a faster/cheaper model for meal suggestion, a warmed static candidate pool, or precomputed decks. The gsex sex-field gate (also part of this flow) remains verified working live.
Honest status: I cannot make the build instant from the client while the endpoint is ~14s/call + throttled; sequential warm + the existing dwell is the best client-side state. Recommend a backend latency decision for the real win.

issues.description: [Claude-raised] Onboarding pre-selects Gender=Male and never requires a choice; gender drives BMR (biological_sex uncollected -> fallback), so non-male users who do not change it get male-BMR calorie targets (~166 kcal high).
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: Shipped: the biological_sex onboarding field + BMR sex-precedence fix (option B) is now live on the Play ALPHA track as v150/1.6.92 (alpha track confirmed [150]), alongside the rest of the batch. Testable on alpha now. Locked by tests on both halves (FE onboarding-calc/findMissingCritical + backend calculate_full_plan). Awaiting your Resolve verdict.

issues.description: [Claude-raised] domain-purple design token renders CYAN, not purple. src/index.css --domain-purple is `190 50% 58%` (hue 190) and the whole d-purple oklch scale uses hue ~195 — both cyan/teal. But the design system (CLAUDE.md) specifies the AI/coaching domain = purple `263 50% 58%`. Net: every AI/coaching surface (coach cards, AI plan buttons, the Suggestions tip button) renders cyan-teal, near-indistinguishable from the teal/health domain (hue 166-175), which collapses two of the four domain colors into one hue.
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: DESIGN/BRAND DECISION NEEDED (owner-only, not changing unilaterally). Is the cyan d-purple intentional (a rebrand of the AI/coaching color away from 263 purple) or a token bug? If INTENTIONAL: I will update the CLAUDE.md design context (it still documents purple 263) so docs match. If a BUG: I will restore --domain-purple to hue ~263 + regenerate the scale, then re-verify all AI surfaces + contrast. Either way teal(166-175) and purple(currently 190) are too close to read as distinct domains. Tell me which and I will execute.

issues.description: [Claude-raised] Frontend Sentry crash reporting is DISABLED in production. .env.production has VITE_SENTRY_DSN= (blank); Vite inlines it as an empty string at build time, so initSentry() (src/lib/sentry.ts:26) hits `if(!dsn) return` and the WHOLE Sentry init -- including the `nyus-frontend@<version>` release tag -- is tree-shaken out. Verified on the v150 dist just shipped: no Sentry init, no release literal anywhere. Net: React render crashes / FE runtime errors are NOT reported in production, and the release-tagging work (commit 2dba89c6) has zero effect. .env.example says prod builds should always set a DSN, so this looks like an unfilled-credential oversight, not intent.
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: OWNER-ONLY (needs the real Sentry DSN). Two paths: (1) ENABLE -- paste the frontend browser DSN from sentry.io (Settings > Projects > nyus > Client Keys) into VITE_SENTRY_DSN in .env.production, then a rebuild + cap sync (next release) starts capturing FE crashes and correlating them to the release tag. The DSN is inlined into the public web bundle -- that is normal/expected for a Sentry browser DSN. (2) INTENTIONALLY OFF -- if you rely only on the backend discord errors channel and do not want FE Sentry, confirm and I will note it in docs and stop flagging. Backend error logging (discord_logger) is unaffected either way.
